==== /.src/effectFnIife.ts (8 errors) ====
    // @effect-v3
    import * as Effect from "effect/Effect"
    
    // Should trigger - IIFE with Effect.fn and generator
    export const iife1 = Effect.fn("test")(function*() {
                         ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
      yield* Effect.succeed(1)
    ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
    })()
    ~~~~
!!! warning TS377011: `Effect.fn` returns a reusable function that can take arguments, but it is invoked immediately here. `Effect.gen` represents the immediate-use form for this pattern with Effect.withSpan("test") piped in the end to maintain tracing spans. effect(effectFnIife)
    
    // Should trigger - IIFE with Effect.fn and name, generator
    export const iife2 = Effect.fn("named")(function*() {
                         ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
      yield* Effect.succeed(1)
    ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
      return 42
    ~~~~~~~~~~~
    })()
    ~~~~
!!! warning TS377011: `Effect.fn` returns a reusable function that can take arguments, but it is invoked immediately here. `Effect.gen` represents the immediate-use form for this pattern with Effect.withSpan("named") piped in the end to maintain tracing spans. effect(effectFnIife)
    
    // Should trigger - IIFE with Effect.fnUntraced
    export const iife3 = Effect.fnUntraced(function*() {
                         ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
      yield* Effect.succeed(1)
    ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
    })()
    ~~~~
!!! warning TS377011: `Effect.fnUntraced` returns a reusable function that can take arguments, but it is invoked immediately here. `Effect.gen` represents the immediate-use form for this pattern. effect(effectFnIife)
    
    // Should trigger - IIFE with Effect.fn and regular arrow function
    export const iife4 = Effect.fn("arrow")(() => Effect.succeed(1))()
                         ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
!!! warning TS377011: `Effect.fn` returns a reusable function that can take arguments, but it is invoked immediately here. `Effect.gen` represents the immediate-use form for this pattern with Effect.withSpan("arrow") piped in the end to maintain tracing spans. effect(effectFnIife)
    
    // Should trigger - IIFE with Effect.fn and regular function expression
    export const iife5 = Effect.fn("func")(function() {
                         ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
      return Effect.succeed(1)
    ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
    })()
    ~~~~
!!! warning TS377011: `Effect.fn` returns a reusable function that can take arguments, but it is invoked immediately here. `Effect.gen` represents the immediate-use form for this pattern with Effect.withSpan("func") piped in the end to maintain tracing spans. effect(effectFnIife)
    
    // Should trigger - IIFE with Effect.fn, generator, and pipe arguments
    export const iife6 = Effect.fn("piped")(function*() {
                         ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
      return yield* Effect.succeed(1)
    ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
    }, Effect.map((n) => n + 1))()
    ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
!!! warning TS377011: `Effect.fn` returns a reusable function that can take arguments, but it is invoked immediately here. `Effect.gen` represents the immediate-use form for this pattern with Effect.withSpan("piped") piped in the end to maintain tracing spans. effect(effectFnIife)
    
    // Should trigger - IIFE with Effect.fnUntraced and pipe arguments
    export const iife7 = Effect.fnUntraced(function*() {
                         ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
      return yield* Effect.succeed(1)
    ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
    }, Effect.map((n) => n + 1))()
    ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
!!! warning TS377011: `Effect.fnUntraced` returns a reusable function that can take arguments, but it is invoked immediately here. `Effect.gen` represents the immediate-use form for this pattern. effect(effectFnIife)
    
    // Should NOT trigger - stored for reuse (generator)
    export const myFn = Effect.fn("myFn")(function*() {
      yield* Effect.succeed(1)
    })
    
    // Should NOT trigger - stored for reuse (regular function)
    export const myFn2 = Effect.fn("myFn2")(() => Effect.succeed(1))
    
    // Should NOT trigger - Effect.gen without Effect.fn
    export const gen = Effect.gen(function*() {
                       ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
      yield* Effect.succeed(1)
    ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
    })
    ~~
!!! suggestion TS377017: This Effect.gen contains a single return statement. effect(unnecessaryEffectGen)
    
    // Should NOT trigger - regular function call
    export const regularFn = (n: number) => Effect.succeed(n)
    export const result = regularFn(1)
